raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "AB 1094 is the first legislation of its kind in the nation focused on LGBTQ violent death data collection.",
    "The bill will enable coroners and medical examiners in six California counties to collect data on an individual's sexual orientation or gender identity in cases of violent death.",
    "Collecting data on sexual orientation and gender identity is critical to understanding violent deaths within the LGBTQ community.",
    "The number of LGBTQ youth who die by suicide or other violent deaths remains unknown due to the lack of broadly collected sexual orientation and gender identity data in the U.S.",
    "Suicide is the second leading cause of death among youth ages 10–24 nationwide.",
    "LGBTQ youth are more than four times more likely to attempt suicide than their straight/cisgender peers.",
    "According to a survey by The Trevor Project, 42 percent of LGBTQ youth seriously considered attempting suicide in the past year, including more than half of transgender and nonbinary youth.",
    "Polling indicates that more than four in five adults (84%) believe it is important to include sexual orientation and gender identity when evaluating suicide and other violent death statistics."
  ]
}
```
